attributes.description: Alerts when any server-level `haproxy.failed_checks` increase. Failed health checks precede the server being marked DOWN.
attributes.name: [HAProxy OTel] Server health check failures

investigation_guide.blob:
## HAProxy server health check failures

### What fired
`haproxy.failed_checks` on a server increased during the window.

### Why it matters
A health-check failure while UP means the check was slow or returned an error. Repeated failures will flip the server to DOWN, removing it from rotation.

### Triage
1. Check the server's application logs and CPU/memory.
2. Inspect network latency to the server.
3. Validate the health-check path is cheap and always available.

### Remediation
- Fix the server or health-check.
- Temporarily increase health-check tolerance if it is overly sensitive.

### Tuning
- Fires on any increase.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-haproxyreceiver.otel-*
// Failed checks (while server UP) — server-level counter only
| WHERE haproxy.failed_checks IS NOT NULL
| WHERE haproxy.service_name != "FRONTEND" AND haproxy.service_name != "BACKEND"
| STATS fail_increase = SUM(INCREASE(haproxy.failed_checks))
  BY haproxy.proxy_name, haproxy.service_name, haproxy.addr
| WHERE fail_increase > 0
| EVAL alert_key = CONCAT(TO_STRING(haproxy.proxy_name), "::", TO_STRING(haproxy.service_name), "::", COALESCE(TO_STRING(haproxy.addr), ""))
| SORT fail_increase DESC
| LIMIT 50
